symptoms.symptom_name: Absolute inability to weight-bear immediately after a low-impact fall

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Weight-bearing failure after a fall in a frail elder is a high-probability marker of proximal femur fracture.
Also causes weight-bearing failure and groin pain; indistinguishable without imaging.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe localised bony pain in right groin/hip and mid-thoracic back

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Groin/hip pain localises to the proximal femur.
Acute focal thoracic bony pain after axial load in osteoporosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Shortened and externally rotated right lower limb

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Shortening and external rotation is the classic sign of a displaced proximal femur fracture — a clinician inspection finding, sealed (cannot be reliably obtained via telehealth).
Posterior dislocation typically presents internally rotated; external rotation favours fracture.

symptoms.symptom_name: Greater-trochanter tenderness and pain on passive internal rotation of the hip

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Localised bony tenderness and pain on hip rotation support proximal femur fracture — clinician exam, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Focal T10-T12 spinous-process bony tenderness with paraspinal spasm

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Focal bony spinal tenderness with spasm on log-roll — clinician exam, sealed; mandates spinal precautions.

symptoms.symptom_name: Established severe osteoporosis (DXA T-score <= -2.5), prior fragility (wrist) fracture, bisphosphonate ceased ~2 years ago

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Severe low BMD and prior fragility fracture markedly increase fracture probability from a low-energy fall; the specific DXA T-score is an investigation value, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Progressive thoracic kyphosis (dowager's hump) with height loss

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pre-existing osteoporotic vertebral wedging raises the pre-test probability of a new compression fracture.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Occult haemorrhage / hypovolaemia from proximal femur fracture
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Autonomic signs may reflect severe pain and/or early blood loss; the AI infers distress from carer report, not from a measured BP/HR.

symptoms.symptom_name: Distal pulses (dorsalis pedis) palpable and intact

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Intact distal pulses make immediate vascular compromise less likely — clinician exam, sealed; does not lower fracture urgency.
Plain radiography (± CT/MRI) is required to confirm/exclude and characterise the fractures — a hospital investigation, never available in real time via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Low-energy fall + weight-bearing failure + groin pain + shortened/externally rotated limb is the classic hip-fracture constellation and a surgical emergency.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Plain radiograph of hip/pelvis (AP pelvis + lateral hip)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line confirmation of a suspected neck-of-femur fracture; performed on arrival at the emergency department.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Thoracolumbar spine imaging (X-ray / CT / MRI as indicated)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the vertebral compression fracture and excludes an unstable or cord-threatening injury.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bloods (FBE, U&E/creatinine, group and hold, CK)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess blood loss, renal function and rhabdomyolysis after a long lie, and prepare for surgery — hospital investigations, not obtainable via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Whole-body / repeat DXA in the community
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not part of acute management; osteoporosis is already established and DXA has no role in the emergency episode.
